items.title: Quick Start
items.content: Get started with Seizn Memory API. Create an API key from the dashboard and make your first request.

items.title: Authentication
items.content: Authenticate requests using the x-api-key header. Never expose API keys in client-side code.

items.title: POST /api/memories - Store Memory
items.content: Store a new memory with content, memory_type, tags, namespace, and scope. Memory types: fact, preference, experience, relationship, instruction.

items.title: GET /api/memories - Search Memories
items.content: Search stored memories using semantic similarity. Parameters: query (required), limit (default 10), threshold (0-1, default 0.7), namespace.

items.title: DELETE /api/memories - Delete Memories
items.content: Delete one or more memories by their IDs. Use comma-separated IDs for bulk deletion.

items.title: POST /api/extract - Extract Memories
items.content: Automatically extract facts, preferences, and instructions from conversations using AI. Models: haiku (faster, cheaper) or sonnet (more accurate).

items.title: POST /api/query - Memory-Augmented Query
items.content: Generate responses using relevant memories as context (RAG). Returns response, memories_used, and model_used.

items.title: Rate Limits
items.content: Free: 1,000 daily calls, 10K memories. Plus: 10,000 daily calls, 100K memories. Pro: 100,000 daily calls, 1M memories. Enterprise: unlimited.

items.title: Error 400 - Bad Request
items.content: Invalid request parameters. Check required fields and data types.

items.title: Error 401 - Unauthorized
items.content: Missing or invalid API key. Ensure x-api-key header is present and valid.

items.title: Error 429 - Too Many Requests
items.content: Rate limit exceeded. Implement exponential backoff or upgrade your plan.

items.title: Error 500 - Server Error
items.content: Internal server error. Contact support if this persists.

items.title: Security & Governance
items.content: Data security features: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS in transit, tenant isolation, API key management, rotation, expiration, usage tracking.

items.title: Data Retention & Compliance
items.content: Export all memories as JSON. Permanent deletion with GDPR forget API. SOC 2, GDPR compliance.

items.title: Python SDK
items.content: pip install seizn. Initialize with Seizn(api_key). Methods: add(), search(), extract().

items.title: JavaScript/TypeScript SDK
items.content: npm install seizn. Initialize with new Seizn({ apiKey }). Methods: add(), search(), extract().

items.title: What is Seizn Memory?
items.content: AI memory infrastructure providing persistent, searchable memory for AI applications with extraction, policy management, key management, and audit logs.

items.title: How is Seizn different from vector databases?
items.content: Seizn is a complete memory system including extraction, type classification, namespace/scope management, API keys, audit logging, SDKs, and governance.

items.title: Why is namespace important?
items.content: Namespace separates memories by project/tenant/environment. Recommended format: org:acme/app:chat/env:prod. Never use default in production.

items.title: When should I use scope?
items.content: user: Global preferences. session: Current conversation context. agent: Rules for specific agent in multi-agent systems.

items.title: What are memory_types?
items.content: fact: unchanging info. preference: user choices. instruction: rules. relationship: connections. experience: events. Most powerful filtering axis.

items.title: How do threshold and limit work?
items.content: limit: Number of results (too low = miss relevant). threshold: 0-1 similarity cutoff (higher = stricter). Start with limit=10, threshold=0.7.

items.title: I'm getting 429 Too Many Requests
items.content: Rate limit hit. Solutions: exponential backoff, server-side queue, cache queries, batch operations, reduce extract frequency.

items.title: Can I use the API key in browser?
items.content: Not recommended - high exposure risk. Call Seizn from server (Route Handler, Worker, serverless). Never expose keys client-side.

items.title: GDPR Forget API
items.content: POST /api/winter/forget - Permanently delete all user data. Right-to-be-forgotten compliance.

items.title: Error Response Format
items.content: All errors include: code (machine-readable), message (human-readable), trace_id, suggested_fix, docs_url. Use trace_id when contacting support.

items.title: AUTH_MISSING_KEY Error
items.content: No API key provided. Fix: Add x-api-key header to your request. Get API key from Dashboard → API Keys.

items.title: AUTH_INVALID_KEY Error
items.content: API key not found or inactive. Fix: Verify key in Dashboard → API Keys. Check for typos or regenerate if needed.

items.title: RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED Error
items.content: Too many requests per minute. Fix: Implement exponential backoff (1s→2s→4s). Check X-RateLimit-Reset header.
